title: Tandoori Chicken with Tamarind Chickpea Salad
summary: Charred tandoori chicken, cooling yoghurt, and a zingy tamarind chickpea salad. Bold, fresh and flavourful.

method.description: Finely grate the garlic into a bowl. Add the tandoori spice mix, Greek yoghurt, a pinch of salt and a few cracks of black pepper. Stir to combine into a marinade.
Butterfly the chicken breasts, then pop them between two sheets of baking parchment. Bash with a rolling pin until they’re about 1cm thick. Add the chicken to the marinade and toss to coat well.

Line a baking tray with foil and lay the chicken breasts flat on top, making sure all the marinade is spread over them. Slide under the grill for 10–15 mins until cooked through and starting to char. If they’re catching too fast, lower the tray or turn the grill down.
Meanwhile, dice the cucumber into 2cm chunks and chop the tomatoes into bite-sized pieces. Pick the mint leaves and discard the stems.
Whisk together the tamarind purée, honey and the juice of the lime in a small bowl. Season with salt to taste and tweak with more lime or honey, if needed.
Toss the chickpeas, cucumber, tomatoes and mint with the tamarind dressing until well coated.
Share the salad onto plates and top with the chicken. Finish with some freshly cracked black pepper and any remaining lime wedges.

chefs.name: Chloe René
chefs.summary: Chloe (@chlo_eat) takes inspiration from her diverse upbringing to create recipes and dishes influenced from cultures around the world. Despite her classical training, Chloe's philosophy in the kitchen revolves around simplicity and home-cooking. She firmly believes that the essence of a great meal lies in the quality of ingredients and the care put into their preparation. Instead of relying on complex and technical processes, Chloe prizes the art of letting ingredients shine through, enhancing their natural flavours with a delicate touch.
